description_long: This object is a compact, tactical karambit knife with a pronounced, sharply curved blade reminiscent of a talon. The blade is single-edged, tapering to a fine point, and finished in a matte black, likely coated for corrosion resistance and reduced reflectivity. The handle is angular and ergonomically shaped, featuring a finger ring at the base for secure grip and enhanced maneuverability, as well as textured panels for improved handling. Two visible screws secure the handle scales, suggesting a full-tang construction for durability. The overall design is utilitarian and modern, with no visible ornamentation, emphasizing function over decoration. The knife is compact, lightweight, and designed for quick, controlled use in close-quarters scenarios.
description: A compact, black karambit knife with a curved blade and ergonomic handle featuring a finger ring and textured grip panels.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full curve of the blade, the finger ring, and the textured handle panels.
description_view_1: Edge-on view highlighting the thinness of the blade and handle, with the knife appearing as a narrow silhouette.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the blade's curve, handle details, and finger ring from the other side.
description_view_3: Edge-on view from the opposite direction, again emphasizing the slim profile of the blade and handle.
attribute_handle_design: angular, ergonomic, black polymer scales, finger ring, textured grip, secured with screws
